title: Grammar Diagnostic Gateway
description: A comprehensive diagnostic assessment to establish your baseline grammar proficiency across all five pathways.
lesson_content_markdown:
# Welcome to the Grammar World Map

Before you begin your journey through the five pathways of academic grammar, we need to establish your current proficiency level.

This diagnostic will assess your understanding across:
- **The Time Matrix**: Verb tenses and aspect
- **The Architecture**: Sentence structure and clauses
- **The Connectors**: Cohesion and transitions
- **The Modifiers**: Articles, adjectives, and precision
- **The Nuance**: Modals, conditionals, and hedging

## Instructions

Complete all 25 questions to the best of your ability. This assessment will help us recommend the most beneficial pathway for you to start with.

There is no time limit. Take your time and demonstrate your current knowledge.

mastery_check.prompt_to_student: Complete the diagnostic assessment below. Select the best answer for each question.

questions.question: The research team _____ the data for three months before publishing their findings.
By the time the conference starts next month, the researchers _____ their paper.
The results _____ by the committee next week.
Since 2010, the university _____ significant improvements in student retention rates.
Had the funding _____ approved earlier, the project would have been completed on schedule.
The professor explained the theory. However, many students still struggled with the concepts.
options: The professor explained the theory, however, many students still struggled with the concepts.
The professor explained the theory however many students still struggled with the concepts.
The professor explained the theory; however many students still struggled with the concepts.

questions.question: The study, _____ was conducted over five years, revealed significant patterns.
_____ the experiment was completed, the scientists discovered an unexpected result.
Only after analyzing the data _____ the researchers realize the significance of their findings.
The study examined student motivation, teacher effectiveness, and _____ curriculum design.
The author argues that technology has improved education. _____, critics point to increased inequality.
The research depends _____ accurate data collection.
The findings were inconclusive. _____, further research is necessary to validate the hypothesis.
_____ the challenges posed by limited funding, the research team completed the study successfully.
The study's methodology was rigorous; _____, the sample size was insufficient for generalization.
_____ information provided in the report is insufficient for drawing conclusions.
There _____ several factors that contribute to climate change.
The committee reviewed _____ before making their decision.
The findings _____ suggest that further research is needed.
Each student must submit _____ assignment by the deadline.
The results _____ indicate a correlation between the variables.
The study _____ significant implications for educational policy.
The researchers avoided _____ any definitive conclusions without further evidence.
The _____ of the data revealed unexpected patterns.
The findings _____ with previous research in the field.
